subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
It's a Wonderful World includes:

- **150 development cards** (projects to draft and build)
- **5 empire cards** (player boards with starting production)
- **170 resource cubes** in 5 types:
  - **White** (Materials)
  - **Black** (Energy)
  - **Green** (Science)
  - **Yellow** (Gold)
  - **Blue** (Exploration)
- **75 character tokens** (Financiers and Generals — used for VP and bonus production)
- **Krystallium tokens** (wild resource)
- **Round tracker** (the game lasts 4 rounds)
- **Score pad**

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Shuffle the **development card deck** and place it centrally.
2. Organize **resource cubes** by type in accessible supply piles.
3. Place **character tokens** (Financiers and Generals) in their own supply piles.
4. Place **Krystallium tokens** in a supply.
5. Set the **round tracker** to Round 1 (the game has 4 rounds).
6. Give each player an **empire card** (all identical for first game; variable powers for advanced play).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player receives their **empire card** which shows:
   - Starting production capabilities (typically 1 of each basic resource)
   - A recycling bonus
   - Slots for placing completed development cards
2. Deal **7 development cards** to each player for the first draft.
3. Players start with **no resources** — production begins after the first drafting phase.
4. In solo mode, a modified deal-and-select mechanism replaces drafting.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
It's a Wonderful World is a **card-drafting engine-building** game where you develop an empire by constructing projects that increase your resource production.

Over **4 rounds**, you draft development cards, then produce resources in a **fixed order** to complete those cards. Completed cards increase your production for future rounds, creating a compounding engine.

Resources are produced in a strict order: **Materials → Energy → Science → Gold → Exploration**. This production order is critical — resources produced early in the chain can immediately be placed on cards, completing them in time to boost later production in the same round.

The player with the most **victory points** from completed cards and character tokens wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each round has 2 phases:

### Phase 1: Drafting
1. Each player receives **7 development cards**.
2. Choose **1 card** to keep. Pass the remaining 6 to the next player.
3. Repeat until all 7 cards are drafted.
4. For each drafted card, you must choose:
   - **Construct it**: Place it in your construction area to build over time.
   - **Recycle it**: Discard it to gain a single resource cube of the type shown on the card's recycling bonus.

### Phase 2: Production
Produce resources in this **strict fixed order**:
1. **Materials (White)**: Each production icon on your empire card and completed cards produces 1 white cube. Place cubes on cards under construction or save them.
2. **Energy (Black)**: Same process.
3. **Science (Green)**: Same process.
4. **Gold (Yellow)**: Same process.
5. **Exploration (Blue)**: Same process.

When a card under construction has **all its required resources** placed on it, it is **immediately completed** — flip it to your empire and add its production to your engine. This can happen mid-production phase!

**Bonus**: During each resource production step, the player who produces the **most** of that resource gains a **Financier or General token** (worth 1 VP and sometimes bonus production).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Drafting Cards
Each card shows:
- **Construction cost**: Resources needed to complete it (cubes of various types)
- **Production bonus**: What the card produces each round once completed
- **Victory points**: VP value when completed
- **Recycling bonus**: What resource you get if you discard the card

### Constructing Cards
- Place drafted cards in your **construction zone**.
- During production, place cubes on cards to fulfill their costs.
- Once fully paid, the card is **completed immediately** and joins your empire.
- There is **no limit** to how many cards you can have under construction.

### Recycling
- Discard a drafted card to receive **one resource cube** of the type shown.
- Recycling is done during the draft phase. The cube goes to your supply to place on constructions.

### Krystallium (Wild Resource)
Some completed cards produce **Krystallium** tokens. Krystallium is a **wild resource** — it can substitute for any resource type when constructing cards.

### Supremacy Bonus
During each production step, the player who produces the **most** of that resource type gains a **character token**:
- Materials/Energy → **General** (1 VP + sometimes military bonus)
- Science/Gold/Exploration → **Financier** (1 VP + sometimes financial bonus)

Ties: all tied players gain the bonus.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Production Order is Key
The fixed production order (Materials → Energy → Science → Gold → Exploration) means:
- A card completed during Materials production adds its Energy/Science/Gold/Exploration production THIS round.
- A card completed during Exploration production won't help until next round.
- **Plan your construction costs** so cards complete early in the production chain.

### Unused Resources
Resources that aren't placed on cards at the **end of the production phase are lost**. Every 5 unused resources convert to 1 Krystallium token.

### Empire Card Variants
In advanced play, each empire card has a **unique side** with different starting production and special abilities, creating asymmetric starts.

### Chaining Completions
If a card completes during production and its new production helps complete another card, that **second card also completes immediately**. Long chains of completions in a single production step are possible and extremely powerful.

### Character Token Scoring
Financiers and Generals each score **1 VP** at game end. Some development cards also have synergies with character tokens.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
After **4 rounds** of drafting and production, calculate final scores:

### Scoring
1. **Completed development cards**: Each completed card shows a VP value (typically 1–10+ VP).
2. **Character tokens**: Each Financier and General is worth **1 VP**.
3. **Uncompleted cards**: Worth **0 points** (no penalty, but wasted investment).

### Winning
The player with the **highest total VP** wins.

### Tiebreaker
If tied, the player with the **most character tokens** wins. If still tied, the player with the **most completed cards** wins.

### Typical Score Ranges
- Beginner games: 40–70 points
- Experienced games: 80–120+ points
- The engine-building nature means scores can vary dramatically based on how well the production chain is optimized.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Production order matters most**: Focus on cards that complete early in the production chain (Materials, Energy) so their output helps this round.
- **Recycle aggressively**: Don't try to build every card you draft. Recycling weak cards for cubes is often better.
- **Build your engine in rounds 1–2**: Early production increases compound over 4 rounds. A card built in round 1 produces 4 times.
- **Watch construction costs**: A card costing 8 resources is rarely worth building in round 3. Prioritize efficient cards early.
- **Don't hoard resources**: Unused cubes are mostly wasted. Place them on constructions even if the card won't complete this round.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Chain completion**: Design your construction pipeline so completing one card immediately triggers another. Round 2 chains can be devastating.
- **Supremacy farming**: Focus production on 1–2 resource types to win supremacy bonuses consistently. Character tokens add up.
- **Pivot late**: Rounds 3–4 are for high-VP cards regardless of production value. You don't need production from cards built in round 4.
- **Resource focus**: Specializing in 2–3 resource types makes your production more efficient than spreading thin.
- **Recycle for timing**: Sometimes recycling a card for 1 cube now is better than building a card worth 3 VP later. Tempo is everything.
- **Krystallium strategy**: Cards producing Krystallium give incredible flexibility. They're rare but powerful — draft them when available.
- **Deny opponents**: If you see a card that perfectly fits an opponent's engine, consider hate-drafting it (or recycling it for the cube).
